svg.text: Transformer with N=2 blocks — every layer shown

g.text: Encoder block 1 (own weights: W¹_Q, W¹_K, W¹_V, W¹_O, W¹_ff, γ¹, β¹)
Encoder block 2 (own weights: W²_Q, W²_K, W²_V, W²_O, W²_ff, γ², β²)

svg.text: Encoder: X₀ → block 1 → X₁ → block 2 → X₂ = E_enc. Sequential, each block refines the representation.
Decoder: Y₀ → block 1 → Y₁ → block 2 → Y₂ → output. Also sequential, but 3 sub-layers per block.
Both decoder blocks receive the SAME E_enc (both orange arrows come from the same source).
Each block has SEPARATE parameters (W¹ ≠ W², γ¹ ≠ γ²). This is why LayerNorm γ/β are not redundant:
Block 1's LayerNorm sees X₀. Block 2's LayerNorm sees X₁ = X₀ + attn₁_delta + FFN₁_delta.
Total unique LayerNorms: encoder has 4 (2 per block × 2 blocks), decoder has 6 (3 per block × 2 blocks) = 10 total.
Each has its own γ and β vectors, because each sees different accumulated residual stream statistics.
